functions.name: concatName
functions.documentation: Concatenates a user-friendly name from the list of provided components. Each argument is converted to string (stringified), trimmed, and the result is concatenated by spaces.

functions.name: strftime
functions.documentation: Formats provided timestamp to string, using a format template specified in POSIX (strftime) notation. Generally recommended instead of formatDateTime.

functions.name: formatDateTime
functions.documentation: Formats provided timestamp to string, using a format template specified in Java SimpleDateFormat notation. Provided mainly for migration from Groovy; use strftime instead.

functions.name: strptime
functions.documentation: Parses provided string to timestamp, using a format template specified in POSIX (strptime) notation. Generally recommended instead of parseDateTime.

functions.name: parseDateTime
functions.documentation: Parses provided string to timestamp, using a format template specified in Java SimpleDateFormat notation. Provided mainly for migration from Groovy; use strptime instead.

functions.name: parseGivenName
functions.documentation: Parses provided string as a person's full name, returning the given name (first name) part.

functions.name: parseFamilyName
functions.documentation: Parses provided string as a person's full name, returning the family name (last name) part.

functions.name: parseAdditionalName
functions.documentation: Parses provided string as a person's full name, returning the additional name (middle name) part.

functions.name: parseNickName
functions.documentation: Parses provided string as a person's full name, returning the nickname part.

functions.name: parseHonorificPrefix
functions.documentation: Parses provided string as a person's full name, returning the honorific prefix (titles before the name) part.

functions.name: parseHonorificSuffix
functions.documentation: Parses provided string as a person's full name, returning the honorific suffix (titles after the name) part.

functions.name: info
functions.documentation: Records formatted message in system logs at INFO level. Curly brackets ({}) act as placeholders for arguments in the message. Returns its argument as output, so it can be placed into a complex expression without breaking its flow.

functions.name: error
functions.documentation: Records formatted message in system logs at ERROR level. Curly brackets ({}) act as placeholders for arguments in the message. Returns its argument as output, so it can be placed into a complex expression without breaking its flow.

functions.name: warn
functions.documentation: Records formatted message in system logs at WARN level. Curly brackets ({}) act as placeholders for arguments in the message. Returns its argument as output, so it can be placed into a complex expression without breaking its flow.

functions.name: debug
functions.documentation: Records formatted message in system logs at DEBUG level. Curly brackets ({}) act as placeholders for arguments in the message. Returns its argument as output, so it can be placed into a complex expression without breaking its flow.

functions.name: trace
functions.documentation: Records formatted message in system logs at TRACE level. Curly brackets ({}) act as placeholders for arguments in the message. Returns its argument as output, so it can be placed into a complex expression without breaking its flow.

functions.name: composeDn
functions.documentation: Creates a valid LDAP distinguished name from the wide range of components. The resulting DN is well-formatted and properly escaped. Note: the DN is not normalized; case of attribute names and white spaces are preserved.

functions.name: composeDnWithSuffix
functions.documentation: Creates a valid LDAP distinguished name from the wide range of components, including a DN suffix at the end. The resulting DN is well-formatted and properly escaped. Note: the DN is not normalized; case of attribute names and white spaces are preserved.

functions.name: hashPassword
functions.documentation: Hashes cleartext password in an (unofficial) LDAP password format. The cleartext password can be provided as a string, polystring, or bytes. Supported algorithms: SSHA, SHA and MD5.

functions.name: determineSingleAttributeValue
functions.documentation: Selects a single value from many LDAP values, based on object DN. E.g. value 'bar' is selected from ['foo','bar','baz'] because that value is present in the given DN.

functions.name: resolveBinary
functions.documentation: Resolves a secret specified by the key, using a provider specified by its name. Returns the secret in binary form (bytes).

functions.name: resolveString
functions.documentation: Resolves a secret specified by the key, using a provider specified by its name. Returns the secret in string form.

functions.name: resolveProtectedString
functions.documentation: Resolves a secret specified by the key, using a provider specified by its name. Returns the secret in protected string form for additional protection.
